verses.text: Among the captives by the river Chebar, the heavens open and Ezekiel sees 'visions of God.' Exile is not abandonment. God meets His prophet in a foreign land, where the throne of heaven is seen even far from home.

Among the captives by the river Chebar, the heavens open and Ezekiel sees 'visions of God.' Exile is not abandonment. God meets His prophet in a foreign land, where the throne of heaven is seen even far from home.

Among the captives by the river Chebar, the heavens open and Ezekiel sees 'visions of God.' Exile is not abandonment. God meets His prophet in a foreign land, where the throne of heaven is seen even far from home.

A storm out of the north, a vast cloud of fire and brightness, and from it four living creatures, each with four faces and four wings. The vision is awesome. God's approach is wrapped in majesty that humbles every beholder.

A storm out of the north, a vast cloud of fire and brightness, and from it four living creatures, each with four faces and four wings. The vision is awesome. God's approach is wrapped in majesty that humbles every beholder.

A storm out of the north, a vast cloud of fire and brightness, and from it four living creatures, each with four faces and four wings. The vision is awesome. God's approach is wrapped in majesty that humbles every beholder.

Their feet gleam like burnished brass, men's hands under their wings, and they move straight forward without turning. Strength and readiness. The servants of God go directly where He sends, swift and unswerving.

Their feet gleam like burnished brass, men's hands under their wings, and they move straight forward without turning. Strength and readiness. The servants of God go directly where He sends, swift and unswerving.

Their feet gleam like burnished brass, men's hands under their wings, and they move straight forward without turning. Strength and readiness. The servants of God go directly where He sends, swift and unswerving.

Each bears four faces — man, lion, ox, and eagle — and goes wherever the spirit directs, never turning aside. Wisdom, power, service, and swiftness in one. They move in perfect obedience to the Spirit's will.

Each bears four faces — man, lion, ox, and eagle — and goes wherever the spirit directs, never turning aside. Wisdom, power, service, and swiftness in one. They move in perfect obedience to the Spirit's will.

Each bears four faces — man, lion, ox, and eagle — and goes wherever the spirit directs, never turning aside. Wisdom, power, service, and swiftness in one. They move in perfect obedience to the Spirit's will.

Among them burn coals of fire and lamps, bright with lightning, as the creatures dart 'as the appearance of a flash of lightning.' Dazzling speed. The ministers of God are full of light and tireless motion.

Among them burn coals of fire and lamps, bright with lightning, as the creatures dart 'as the appearance of a flash of lightning.' Dazzling speed. The ministers of God are full of light and tireless motion.

Beside each is a wheel, 'a wheel in the middle of a wheel,' with rings high and 'full of eyes round about.' Providence is intricate. God's workings interlock and see all, missing nothing in their course.

Beside each is a wheel, 'a wheel in the middle of a wheel,' with rings high and 'full of eyes round about.' Providence is intricate. God's workings interlock and see all, missing nothing in their course.

Beside each is a wheel, 'a wheel in the middle of a wheel,' with rings high and 'full of eyes round about.' Providence is intricate. God's workings interlock and see all, missing nothing in their course.
When the creatures move, the wheels move, 'for the spirit of the living creature was in the wheels.' One Spirit drives all. Heaven and earth turn together under the single will of God.
When the creatures move, the wheels move, 'for the spirit of the living creature was in the wheels.' One Spirit drives all. Heaven and earth turn together under the single will of God.
When the creatures move, the wheels move, 'for the spirit of the living creature was in the wheels.' One Spirit drives all. Heaven and earth turn together under the single will of God.
Above their heads stretches a firmament like awesome crystal, and from above it comes a voice. Order and command. Over all the living motion stands a fixed throne from which God speaks.

Above their heads stretches a firmament like awesome crystal, and from above it comes a voice. Order and command. Over all the living motion stands a fixed throne from which God speaks.

Above the firmament is a throne like sapphire, and on it One like a man, glorious as the rainbow — 'the appearance of the likeness of the glory of the LORD.' Ezekiel falls on his face. To glimpse God's glory is to be laid low in worship.
